description: Pentium and P6-era instruction additions, covering the original Pentium (1993), Pentium Pro / P6 (1995), and SSE/SSE2 extensions (Pentium III / Pentium 4). Includes CPUID, RDTSC, CMPXCHG8B, fast system-call instructions (SYSENTER/SYSEXIT), memory-fence instructions, PAUSE, and UD2.

opcodes.description: Query processor identification and feature information; input in EAX (and ECX for sub-leaves); output in EAX, EBX, ECX, EDX

instructions.description: Returns processor identification and feature information based on the value of EAX (and sometimes ECX) into EAX, EBX, ECX, and EDX.

opcodes.description: Read 64-bit Time-Stamp Counter into EDX:EAX (or RDX:RAX in 64-bit mode)

instructions.description: Read Time-Stamp Counter: loads the 64-bit timestamp counter into EDX:EAX (or RDX:RAX in 64-bit mode).
instructions.notes: May require RDTSCP (0x0F 0x01 0xF9) for serialized reads on modern CPUs.

opcodes.description: Compare EDX:EAX with 64-bit memory; if equal store ECX:EBX to memory, else load memory into EDX:EAX

instructions.description: Compares EDX:EAX with a 64-bit memory value; if equal, stores ECX:EBX; otherwise loads the memory value into EDX:EAX.
instructions.notes: Use LOCK prefix for atomicity. On x86-64, CMPXCHG16B (REX.W + 0x0F 0xC7 /1) compares RDX:RAX with 128-bit memory.

opcodes.description: Read Model-Specific Register ECX into EDX:EAX

instructions.description: Read Model-Specific Register: reads the MSR specified by ECX into EDX:EAX. Requires ring-0.

opcodes.description: Write EDX:EAX into Model-Specific Register ECX

instructions.description: Write Model-Specific Register: writes EDX:EAX into the MSR specified by ECX. Requires ring-0.

opcodes.description: Fast 32-bit system call using MSRs SYSENTER_CS/ESP/EIP; transfers to ring-0

instructions.description: Fast 32-bit system-call instruction using MSRs (SYSENTER_CS, SYSENTER_ESP, SYSENTER_EIP).
instructions.notes: Superseded by SYSCALL on x86-64 platforms. Still used on some 32-bit systems.

opcodes.description: Return from fast 32-bit system call (companion to SYSENTER); transfers to ring-3
Undefined Instruction: guaranteed to raise Invalid Opcode exception (#UD)

instructions.description: Undefined Instruction: guaranteed to raise an Invalid Opcode exception (#UD). Used for deliberate fault generation and as a trap in testing/debugging.

opcodes.description: Store fence: all preceding store operations complete before any following stores (serializes stores)

instructions.description: Store Fence: ensures all preceding store operations are completed before any following stores.

opcodes.description: Load fence: all preceding load operations complete before any following loads (serializes loads)

instructions.description: Load Fence: ensures all preceding load operations are completed before any following loads.

opcodes.description: Memory fence: all preceding memory operations (loads and stores) complete before any following ones

instructions.description: Memory Fence: ensures all preceding memory operations are completed before any following ones (full store/load fence).

opcodes.description: Spin-wait hint: reduces power consumption and improves SMT performance in spin loops; decoded as REP NOP on older CPUs

instructions.description: Hint to the processor that it is executing a spin-wait loop, reducing power consumption and improving performance on HyperThreading/SMT systems.
instructions.notes: Encoded as REP prefix (0xF3) + NOP (0x90). Decoded as NOP on CPUs that do not support PAUSE semantics.
